q01.name: The Dragon's Tithe
q01.desc: A whole valley pays it tribute and calls that peace. The crown calls it rebellion with wings.
q01.epilogue: The valley keeps its harvest for the first year in living memory; the tithe-barns are rebuilt as granaries.

q02.name: The Giant of the Border Marches
q02.desc: Border forts flattened, garrisons walking home weaponless. The marshal wants the marches quiet again.
q02.epilogue: The forts are re-garrisoned; the marches go quiet, and stay so.

q03.name: Press-Gang Trouble
q03.desc: A rival boss's press-gang is stealing crews off the night shift. The foreman pays for it stopped, no questions on the how.
q03.epilogue: The night crews walk home unescorted; the rival boss's tower stands quiet -- under new management within the week.

q04.name: The Engine Gone Feral
q04.desc: They built it too big and fed it too well, and now the lower works belong to it.
q04.epilogue: The lower works are re-lit gallery by gallery; the bosses are already arguing about building it again, bigger.

traits.quirk: has a child (with them: Rivet, age 12)

q05.name: The Giant of the Border Marches
q05.desc: Border forts flattened, garrisons walking home weaponless. The marshal wants the marches quiet again.
q05.epilogue: The forts are re-garrisoned; the marches go quiet, and stay so.

q06.name: Bandits on the King's Road
q06.desc: Wagons stripped, a toll of blood at the fords. The reeve pays for the road made safe.
q06.epilogue: The fords run quiet again; carters raise a cup to the party at every wayhouse on the road.

traits.quirk: has a child (with them: Veld, age 8)

q07.name: The Restless Crypt
q07.desc: The churchyard dead are not staying put. The parish purse opens for whoever settles them.
q07.epilogue: The graves are re-consecrated at dusk; the parish sleeps its first whole night in weeks.

q08.name: Deserters Turned Raiders
q08.desc: Soldiers who kept their steel and lost their oaths, living off the villages they once guarded.
q08.epilogue: The recovered blades go back to the armory; the villages post no watch tonight.

q09.name: The Restless Crypt
q09.desc: The churchyard dead are not staying put. The parish purse opens for whoever settles them.
q09.epilogue: The graves are re-consecrated at dusk; the parish sleeps its first whole night in weeks.

q10.name: Wardens Gone Rogue
q10.desc: A warden-band that answers to no council now, taxing the forest roads at arrowpoint.
q10.epilogue: The rogue lodge is unroofed and left to the moss; the forest roads charge no toll but weather.

q11.name: The Blighted Glade
q11.desc: Something soured the heartwood; what lives there now bites. The wardens want it cut out.
q11.epilogue: Green returns to the glade's edge within the season; what soured the heartwood is burned and sung over.

traits.quirk: has a child (with them: Heian, age 12)

q12.name: Wardens Gone Rogue
q12.desc: A warden-band that answers to no council now, taxing the forest roads at arrowpoint.
q12.epilogue: The rogue lodge is unroofed and left to the moss; the forest roads charge no toll but weather.

q13.name: Beasts Maddened by the Blight
q13.desc: Boar and bear alike gone wrong-eyed, tearing through the outlying groves.
q13.epilogue: The groves go quiet again; the keeper plants a sapling for every beast that had to die.

q14.name: The Troll Under the Pass
q14.desc: The mountain road pays tribute in carts and drivers. The clans want the pass opened.
q14.epilogue: Carts roll the pass by the next moon; drivers still spit at the cave mouth for luck.

q15.name: Wyvern on the Crags
q15.desc: A shadow over the herds, and herders who point at the high crags and will not climb.
q15.epilogue: The herders climb again; the skull goes up on a spear above the trail, warning and boast at once.

q16.name: The Proving Hunt
q16.desc: The clan sets a beast worth a name. Bring back its hide and the clan pays in iron and standing.
q16.epilogue: The hide hangs in the long-tent and the party's name is spoken at the fire -- iron and standing, paid in full.

q17.name: Rival Warband
q17.desc: A rival clan's raiders cut the herd trails. The warchief pays for them driven off or dead.
q17.epilogue: The rival clan sends a peace-gift of salt and knives; the herd trails run open.

q18.name: Press-Gang Trouble
q18.desc: A rival boss's press-gang is stealing crews off the night shift. The foreman pays for it stopped, no questions on the how.
q18.epilogue: The night crews walk home unescorted; the rival boss's tower stands quiet -- under new management within the week.

q19.name: Scrap-Hounds Loose in the Works
q19.desc: The bosses' hound-pack slipped its chains and dens in the gearworks. Pay docked for every eaten shift-worker.
q19.epilogue: The works run a full shift without a scream; the foreman docks nobody, this once.

q20.name: The Blighted Glade
q20.desc: Something soured the heartwood; what lives there now bites. The wardens want it cut out.
q20.epilogue: Green returns to the glade's edge within the season; what soured the heartwood is burned and sung over.

traits.quirk: has a child (with them: Naal, age 11)

q21.name: Webs in the Canopy
q21.desc: The high walkways are silked over and the wardens who went up have not come down.
q21.epilogue: The silk is cut away and the walkways rehung; the lost wardens are carried down for their rites.

giver.name: Thamior
giver.role: the master of the high walkways

q28.epilogue: The rival clan sends a peace-gift of salt and knives; the herd trails run open.

q29.name: Spiders in the Undercity
q29.desc: The vent shafts are webbed shut and the air below is going bad. Small pay, small heroes welcome.
q29.epilogue: Air moves in the undercity again; small pay, but the vent-warden's word is now good in every burrow.
